mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during periods of stable inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based domestic demand expansion, boosting corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% year-over-year, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in improved corporate margins.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during periods of rising input costs, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of instances when INDPRO rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 210.0, elevated inflation erodes real corporate profits and forces tighter financial conditions, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of periods above the threshold, or the predictive relationship vanishes when real interest rates are negative.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods with positive payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms synchronized demand expansion across production and consumption channels, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns when FEDFUNDS increases by >0.5% in the same quarter, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing activity and weakening business investment, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of accommodative monetary policy.
